title: After 'solving' 8 wars, Trump gives 10 interviews to explain initiating 9th
content: President Trump offered multiple justifications for the air campaign against Iran, including a preemptive strike against its supreme leader. He cited intelligence of assassination plots, election interference, and an imminent nuclear threat, though evidence remains unrevealed. Trump's strategy aims to control the narrative, presenting varied reasons to different outlets.

title: US closes 3 Gulf embassies, urges Americans to leave Middle East; what's Trump planning?
content: US embassies in Saudi Arabia and Kuwait have closed following drone attacks, with Americans urged to leave 14 Middle Eastern nations. President Trump signaled intensified military action against Iran, not ruling out ground troops. The conflict has widened, impacting oil supplies and causing market turmoil, with significant casualties reported.

title: 'Thursday night is his night': Will Jos Buttler boss India at Wankhede in semifinal?
content: Buttler had a difficult ODI World Cup in India in 2023 and has not made a significant impact so far in the ongoing T20 World Cup. This comes in a country where he has scored heavily in the IPL, including the 2025 season.

title: Iran burns, China waits: Why Beijing won’t fight for Tehran
content: Despite Iran's critical economic reliance on China, Beijing's response to strikes on its leader was limited to verbal condemnation and calls for de-escalation.  China prioritizes its broader economic interests and balanced regional ties over defending Tehran militarily, demonstrating a strategic calculus that favors diplomacy and deniability over direct intervention or security guarantees.

title: Tech employees across America send open letter to Pentagon on Anthropic
content: Hundreds of tech employees have signed an open letter urging the Department of War and Congress to withdraw Anthropic's "supply chain risk" designation. This action followed a failed contract negotiation, leading to a presidential directive to cease using Anthropic's technology. The letter argues this precedent punishes American innovation and urges a resolution through commercial channels.

title: Middle East on boil: Russia says ready to help India with energy supplies in case of disruption
content: Russia has offered energy supply support to India amidst Middle East tensions. New Delhi, while monitoring the escalating Gulf crisis, assures sufficient crude oil and petroleum product reserves. Diversified sourcing and strategic stockpiles provide a buffer, with a 24/7 control room ensuring national energy security and protecting citizens in the region.

title: In a historic first, Indian women's cricket team nominated for Laureus honour
content: India’s women’s cricket team has been nominated for the 2026 Laureus World Team of the Year Award after winning their maiden World Cup title. They are the first women’s cricket team to earn this honour, highlighted by a record 339-run chase against Australia. The winners will be announced in Madrid on April 20 by the Laureus World Sports Academy jury.

title: 75+ happy Holi messages, greetings, wishes and quotes for 2026
content: Holi 2026 is approaching. Avoid generic messages this year. The article provides over 75 unique Holi wishes for family, friends, and colleagues.

title: ​​World's two richest men who were hit most by stock market bloodbath triggered by Anthropic's new AI models
content: Anthropic's AI tools triggered a stock market dip, impacting tech giants. While most billionaires saw their fortunes fluctuate, Elon Musk experienced a remarkable surge, adding $64 billion in February alone. He's now on track to potentially be the first person to achieve a trillion-dollar net worth, with SpaceX's IPO on the horizon.

title: Phone networks, traffic lights hacked: How US, Israel tracked & killed Khamenei
content: In a dramatic operation, Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in coordinated US-Israeli strikes. Years of meticulous surveillance, including hacked traffic cameras and disrupted phone networks, provided crucial intelligence. This enabled a precise strike during a rare meeting, marking a significant escalation driven by political decisions and advanced technological capabilities.

title: 'Resisted Babar's inclusion': Pak selector resigns after team's poor performance in T20 WC
content: Senior selection committee member Aleem Dar has resigned from the Pakistan Cricket Board. This follows the national team's failure to reach the T20 World Cup semifinals. Dar reportedly cited concerns over head coach Mike Hesson's influence in team selection. Pakistan's disappointing performance has led to player fines. Financial benefits will now be tied to performance.

title: Assam Assembly election: Congress releases first list of 42 candidates; fields Gaurav Gogoi in Jorhat
content: The Congress party has unveiled its initial list of 42 candidates for the upcoming Assam Assembly elections, launching its campaign early. Key figures like Gaurav Gogoi and Debabrata Saikia will contest from Jorhat and Nazira respectively, as the party seeks to regain power after a significant hiatus.

title: OpenAI is changing its contract with Pentagon; CEO Sam Altman says: I would rather go to jail than…
content: OpenAI is amending its Pentagon deal after critics said the original contract didn't actually ban AI-powered mass surveillance of Americans. CEO Sam Altman added explicit language prohibiting domestic surveillance—including through commercially purchased data—and confirmed NSA use is off the table for now. He also said he'd "rather go to jail" than follow an unconstitutional order, and urged the DoD to offer Anthropic the same revised terms.

title: Ayesha Khan breaks silence on trolling over shooting Dhurandhar song Shararat on her periods
content: Ayesha Khan has addressed the backlash she faced after revealing that she shot the dance track Shararat while on her period in Aditya Dhar’s espionage thriller Dhurandhar. The actress said she was taken aback by how a normal confession turned into meme material online.

title: Middle East crisis: How much oil, LPG, LNG reserves does India have?
content: India’s position carries inherent pressure points given that the country imports roughly 90% of its crude requirement, with around 45–50% sourced from the Middle East.

title: 'Missile, drone attack imminent': US embassy in Saudi Arabia issues security alert
content: The US Embassy in Saudi Arabia issued a warning of imminent missile and UAV attacks over Dhahran, advising American citizens to shelter immediately. The alert follows escalating regional tensions after US and Israeli airstrikes on Iranian sites, leading to retaliatory attacks by Iran on Israeli territory and US bases in Gulf states.

title: Why will India be knocked out if rain washes out T20 WC semi-final?
content: The T20 World Cup 2026 semi-finals feature India vs England in Mumbai and South Africa vs New Zealand in Kolkata. Though the forecast is clear, rain concerns remain after earlier washouts. The ICC has reserve days and extra time in place. If a semi-final is abandoned, the higher Super 8 team advances — meaning England and South Africa would qualify.

title: Katrina Kaif and Vicky Kaushal celebrate first holi after son Vihaan's birth,
content: Katrina Kaif and Vicky Kaushal celebrated their first Holi as parents to baby Vihaan with an intimate family gathering. Katrina shared a joyful group photo on Instagram, while Vicky recently spoke about fatherhood, praising Katrina as a superhero and warrior during her pregnancy and motherhood. He described fatherhood as a transformative and grounding experience.

title: ISL: Mohun Bagan hit with stadium closure for 'spectator misconduct'
content: Mohun Bagan Super Giants have been fined and hit with a full stadium closure for their upcoming game against Odisha FC on March 6. The Kolkata giants have been found guilty of 'spectator misconduct' during their season opener against the Kerala Blasters on February 14.

title: US-Iran war: Indian students moved out of Tehran
content: The Indian Embassy in Tehran has moved most Indian students to safer areas outside the city. This action follows heightened risks due to ongoing US-Iran tensions. The embassy is providing transportation, food, and accommodation. Other Indian nationals in Iran are advised to stay indoors and avoid protests. Emergency helplines are active as the security situation is closely monitored.

title: 72 hours, 12 nations: How Iran's 'cheap drones' are unleashing havoc across Middle East
content: Iran is shifting its retaliation strategy from dramatic displays to sustained pressure, utilizing cheap drones to exhaust air defenses and rattle populations across Israel and Gulf states. This approach aims to drain expensive interceptor inventories and destabilize the region, making the conflict a drawn-out endurance test rather than a single event.

title: TN double murder: 2 hacked to death outside tea shop; 5 injured, heavy police deployed
content: Tragedy struck Perumpathu village as a brutal attack left two dead, including a guest worker, and five injured. A gang reportedly hacked seven individuals outside a tea shop late Monday night.  The victims, identified as Trinath Kata and John Mark, died instantly.  Villagers protested, demanding swift arrests. Police have secured six individuals as investigations continue amidst a tense atmosphere.

title: US-Iran war: PM Modi speaks to Gulf leaders, discusses security of Indians
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke with the Sultan of Oman and the Crown Prince of Kuwait. Discussions focused on escalating Middle East tensions and the safety of Indians in these nations. India is actively engaging with Gulf partners. The nation prioritizes the well-being of its citizens abroad. India calls for restraint and de-escalation in the region.

title: Sapthami Gowda demands dignity: 'Kantara' star slams objectionable paparazzi shots
content: Megastar Chiranjeevi showered immense praise on his son Ram Charan's upcoming film 'Peddi', calling the new song 'Rai Rai Raa Raa' sheer fire. The song, featuring Charan's graceful dance and electrifying presence, has generated significant buzz. With A.R. Rahman's chartbuster first song and a strong cast, 'Peddi' is set for a grand release on April 30, 2026, with expectations soaring.

title: IPL 2026: RCB to play five home games at Chinnaswamy Stadium
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru will host five of their seven home matches at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru for the 2026 IPL season. This comes after extensive coordination with authorities to ensure matches can be held in the city. The remaining two home fixtures will be played in Raipur.

title: 'Energy supplies robust', says govt amid Middle East crisis, control rooms set up
content: India possesses ample energy reserves to navigate current Middle East tensions, assured Petroleum Minister Hardeep Puri. He highlighted the nation's commitment to energy availability, affordability, and sustainability, addressing concerns over potential supply disruptions. This assurance comes as global oil routes face risks, impacting major Asian economies reliant on Middle Eastern crude.

title: Virat Kohli walks with son Akaay in London, video goes viral - Watch
content: Virat Kohli, currently on a break from cricket, was spotted with his son Akaay in London. The former captain is prioritizing family time and privacy for his children.  Kohli also shared Holi wishes on social media and is gearing up for his return in the IPL 2026 season with RCB.

title: From CAA to Kashmir: Why India is silent over Khamenei's demise
content: India's measured response to Iran's supreme leader's assassination, focusing on regional de-escalation, has sparked domestic criticism. While condemning Iranian strikes on Gulf allies, New Delhi has avoided direct condemnation of the killing, prioritizing national interests like energy security and diaspora welfare. This stance contrasts with past criticisms of Iran's internal affairs.

title: Stress, sleep and screen time: The lifestyle triangle behind weight gain
content: In today's fast-paced world, factors such as chronic stress, sleepless nights, and excessive screen time play a pivotal role in weight gain, especially around the midsection. This triad of lifestyle habits not only leads to poor nutrition and a lack of physical activity but also heightens the risk of conditions like diabetes and heart disease for both young and old.

title: India gets 93rd GM: Mother quit job for chess — Making of Aarav Dengla
content: Sixteen-year-old Aarav Dengla has become India’s 93rd Grandmaster (GM). Guided by his mother Shipra, who quit her job to support his career, Aarav rose rapidly from prodigy to champion. Initially trained by GM Pravin Thipsay and then under Vishnu Prasanna, and balancing rigorous academics, he sealed his final GM norm in 2026, emerging as Mumbai’s third Grandmaster.

title: Iran earthquake: 4.3-magnitude tremor hits Gerash region of Iran
content: A 4.3 magnitude earthquake shook the southern Iranian city of Gerash. The tremor was reported at a depth of 10 kilometers. Initial reports indicate no casualties or damage from the earthquake. Further details are awaited on this developing story.

title: From Khamenei's compound to nuclear site: Satellite images show aftermath of US-Israel strikes
content: High-resolution satellite images reveal extensive destruction across Iran following US-Israeli strikes on nuclear, military, and leadership sites. Operation Epic Fury targeted several locations in Iran, including Natanz nuclear facility and Ayatollah Khamenei's compound, with reports indicating that the Supreme Leader's death. Khamenei was killed in one of the initial strikes on Saturday.

title: EXCLUSIVE | 'It was a responsibility': BCCI prez. Manhas on J&K's rise
content: Mithun Manhas reflects on his emotions when Paras Dogra-led Jammu and Kashmir won the 2025-26 Ranji Trophy title. He traces J&K’s revival to the 2021 BCCI-appointed sub-committee, credits stability and discipline for the turnaround, backs Auqib Nabi as a product of domestic grind, and expresses full faith in Suryakumar Yadav-led India to deliver in front of home crowd in the T20 World Cup.

title: Three Indian seafarers killed amid Middle East tensions; Centre issues advisory
content: Tragic news from the Middle East as three Indian seafarers lost their lives and one was injured in recent incidents involving foreign-flagged vessels. Amidst escalating US-Iran tensions, maritime authorities are urging extreme caution, enhanced security, and thorough risk assessments for all voyages in the region.  The remaining crew are safe, with authorities coordinating support.

title: How Iran's strikes are putting Dubai's 'safe-haven' reputation under strain
content: Dubai, once a symbol of Middle Eastern stability, now faces unprecedented threats after Iranian missile and drone strikes targeted its airport and iconic landmarks. The attacks shattered the city's aura of invincibility, raising serious concerns for its economic model and the safety of millions of residents, particularly the large Indian diaspora.

title: 'No better feeling': 149 Indians fly home from Dubai on Air India
content: Air India successfully repatriated 149 passengers and 8 crew members from Dubai to Delhi, marking the first international arrival by an Indian carrier. This operation comes as Dubai's airports begin partial resumption of limited flight operations after days of disruption due to escalating regional tensions and airspace restrictions.

title: AFC Women's Asian Cup: From COVID-19 heartbreak, India back on merit against the continent's best
content: After a COVID-induced withdrawal in 2022, India's women's football team has qualified for the AFC Women's Asian Cup on merit for the first time since 2003. Under new coach Amelia Valverde, the team faces a tough challenge in Australia against Japan, Vietnam, and Chinese Taipei, aiming to compete and showcase their capabilities on the international stage. The dream is clear: qualify for the FIFA Women's World Cup.

title: Big combine shakeups in the top 64? Reid projects Rounds 1-2 with his new NFL mock draft
content: Now that the 2026 combine is in the books, how could the draft shake out? Jordan Reid projects the first two rounds -- including two trades.

title: Sources: Jets place franchise tag on star RB Hall
content: The Jets placed their franchise tag on running back Breece Hall ahead of Tuesday's deadline, sources told ESPN.

title: Trae tossed from bench ahead of Wizards debut
content: Trae Young, who is set to make his Wizards debut Thursday vs. Utah, was ejected for leaving the bench in Monday's loss to Houston.

title: Source: Mack to play in '26, explore all FA options
content: Outside linebacker Khalil Mack, who had mulled retirement, will play in 2026 and explore all options in free agency, a source told ESPN, confirming a report by NFL Network.

title: Falcons get 5th European game, this time Madrid
content: The NFL announced Tuesday that the Falcons would be playing a game in Madrid, one of the NFL's nine international games this coming season.

title: Rahm accuses World Tour of 'extorting' players
content: LIV's Jon Rahm on Tuesday accused the DP World Tour of attempting to "extort" golfers and said he won't commit to competing in six events on the European tour each season.

title: Vertigo symptoms delay McLean's arrival for WBC
content: Mets pitcher Nolan McLean, whose arrival to camp ahead of the WBC has been delayed due to vertigo-like symptoms, is confident he'll join Team USA later this week.

title: Madrid's Rodrygo (torn ACL) to miss World Cup
content: Real Madrid on Tuesday confirmed that forward Rodrygo has a torn ACL and a lateral meniscus rupture on his right knee, injuries that are likely to mean he will miss this summer's World Cup with Brazil.

title: 2-time gold medalist to swim in Enhanced Games
content: Two-time Olympic gold medalist Hunter Armstrong plans to swim in the PED-friendly Enhanced Games while still pursuing hopes to compete in the 2028 Olympics.

title: 🏀 Why Billups could be facing decades in prison
content: After a Hall of Fame NBA career, here's what we know about Billups' connections to Mafia-affiliated poker games and illegal gambling.

title: Projecting six win-win NHL trades ahead of Friday's deadline
content: How many blockbusters will occur before 3 p.m. ET on Friday? Here are six deals we think teams should make.

title: Men's Bracketology: Miami (Ohio) or Auburn? Sorry, Bruce Pearl -- it's the RedHawks
content: The RedHawks are two wins away from an undefeated regular season while the Tigers are barely above .500.

title: The 357 teams that won't win the men's national title -- and the 8 that could
content: It's a no-brainer if one of these eight teams wins in April. For the remaining 357? Better luck next year.

title: Women's Bracketology: Welcome to the top 16, Minnesota
content: Sunday's reveal put a new team in the top 16. The start of Champ Week will bring more answers as the bracket comes into focus.

title: From 50/50 to Cy Young? Shohei Ohtani is a man on a mission
content: Dodgers teammates say MLB's most singular star has set his sights on another history-making achievement.

title: Ranking MLB positions: Which spots on the diamond have the most -- and least -- projected production?
content: From "Generation Shortstop" to a power position that has lost its pop, here's how every area stacks up for 2026.

title: First title in 40 years? Inside the Mets' winter makeover
content: Saying goodbye to the team's four longest-tenured players was tough. But New York is turning the page.

title: Barnwell stacks the best defensive players available in free agency next week: Who will get big deals?
content: With one week until NFL free agency, let's put defensive players into six tiers based on their potential offseason contracts.

title: World Cup Power Rankings: Who are the faves with 100 days to go?
content: With 100 days until the 2026 World Cup begins, our reporters ranked the top 15 contenders for this summer's top prize as they see it. Did your national team make the cut? And who do we think will challenge hardest?

title: Football is life! 'Dani Rojas' from 'Ted Lasso' is on trial at a USL club
content: Cristo Fernández, beloved by fans the world over for his role as Dani Rojas in Ted Lasso, looks set to make his soccer dreams a reality as he trials with El Paso.

title: The curious case of Sawyer Robertson, Mike Leach's last quarterback
content: It took the former Baylor signal-caller years to get comfortable running an offense. Will he be able to make things work in the NFL?

title: How many future Hall of Famers are playing in the 2026 WBC?
content: From the locks to the long shots to those in between, we count Cooperstown candidates in this year's World Baseball Classic.

title: Plum: WNBA's CBA offer is 'win,' but talks go on
content: Kelsey Plum said the CBA offer from the league to the WNBPA is a "significant win," but negotiations should continue and "a strike would be the worst thing for both sides."

title: The craziest F1 start ever? Why Australian Grand Prix might see chaos from the start
content: Grand prix starts have always held the potential for chaos, but this year there's almost guaranteed drama when the lights go out in Australia on Sunday. ESPN explains why.

title: Has Aston Martin's F1 super team turned into a disaster team?
content: Fernando Alonso described Martin as the "team of the future" -- but it heads to Australia in disarray.

title: Boys' 2026 recruiting class rankings: Duke is the new No. 1, UNC enters at No. 7
content: Duke is poised for a third straight No. 1 class. Arkansas comes in at No. 2, and UNC makes its 2026 rankings debut.

title: The Premier League has stopped being fun. Here's how one brave coach could fix it
content: The Premier League just isn't as fun as it used to be. But ESPN's Ryan O'Hanlon has figured out a way to fix it, if one coach is brave enough.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Bruno Fernandes plans Man United future talks
content: Bruno Fernandes has shown no signs of wanting to leave Manchester United, while Casemiro has urged the club to sign Bruno Guimarães. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Boxing divisional rankings: Navarrete keeps top spot with dominant win over Nunez
content: Emanuel Navarette dominated Eduardo Nunez to keep his top spot at 130 pounds. Here are the latest ESPN divisional rankings.

title: Takeaways: Only one surprising outcome at Elimination Chamber
content: The title matchups for WrestleMania 42 are nearly set, but there is only one pairing that we didn't see coming.

title: Draft guide: Rankings, tips, strategy, stats and more
content: All the rankings, projections, cheat sheets, strategy and analysis you need for the 2026 fantasy baseball season.

title: Pickups: Add Dejounte Murray, Precious Achiuwa before it's too late
content: Every week in fantasy basketball, there are available players who can help fill holes in your roster. Here are the ones to add for the week ahead.

title: Saba Shawl becomes first Kashmiri woman Superintendent of Srinagar Central Jail
content: She has already served in the administration department at the Central Jail in Kot Balwal, Jammu, and the Kupwara Jail.

title: Meta takes down social media handles of prominent Jammu and Kashmir news outlets
content: Such actions not only silence legitimate voices but also undermine the fundamental right to freedom of expression, says Mehbooba

title: Workers flag glitches in job scheme app
content: Mazdoor Kisan Shakti Sangathan reports widespread failures in National Mobile Monitoring System’s facial recognition update; the government says over 22 lakh workers successfully used the feature on Tuesday

title: Manipur CM flags off multi-community national integration tour
content: Twenty-four youths from Meitei, Kuki, Naga and Pangal (Muslim) communities to travel across five cities in first such initiative since 2023 ethnic violence

title: Left parties slam government’s silence on Iran attack, Khamenei killing
content: CPI(M) leader Brinda Karat says Prime Minister Narendra Modi has “bowed” in front of U.S. President Donald Trump and has held Israel Prime Minister “Benjamin Netanyahu’s bloodied hands”

title: BJP leader Ravi Shankar Prasad to head Lok Sabha’s Privileges Committee
content: The panel examines cases referred by the Speaker and submits reports on matters where breaches of privilege are alleged

title: Boy lynched over suspicion of stealing goats in Odisha
content: When the victims were passing by motor cycle, they were caught hold of and asked about stolen goats, police  said; the two victims were then then thrashed with sticks

title: Peak load of power in Telangana crosses 18K MW mark, braces for higher demand in summer
content: Deputy CM Bhatti Vikramarka compliments engineers, officials and staff of power utilities for handling the unrestricted power demand

title: Healthcare workers’ use of GenAI platforms might risk leaks of sensitive patient data: study
content: Organisations should go in for company-approved apps and relevant security tools to address internal threats, says expert

title: 3,000 sanitation workers remove waste after Pongala, separate squads retrieve bricks to be used for Corporation’s housing projects
content: Although the cleanup drive started early in the afternoon, waste was not removed from several parts including the bypass area near Chackai, Fort and Vazhuthacaud even at night, unlike the previous years

title: BJP names two from eastern Assam as RS poll candidates
content: The nomination of Minister Jogen Mohan and MLA Terash Gowala is significant as they are from communities demanding Scheduled Tribe status

title: Heavy security deployment, effective control mark incident-free Pongala
content: Throughout the day, most arterial roads, barring those leading to Attukal Bhagavathy Temple, remained clear as the police effectively regulated traffic to ensure smooth vehicular movement

title: With hotelier Dilip Ray in the fray, Rajya Sabha contest in Odisha sparks interest
content: Following his expulsion from the Biju Janata Dal in 2002, the former Union Coal Minister had pulled off a surprise win in the election to the Upper House as an independent candidate

title: DMK allots one Rajya Sabha seat to DMDK
content: The agreement signed by Mr. Stalin and Mr. Sudheesh said the seat-sharing committee would decide on the number of Assembly seats to be allotted to the party.

title: Flames of devotion burn bright at Attukal Pongala
content: On Tuesday, the penultimate day of the 10-day Attukal Pongala festival, the feverish excitement that had gripped the city for days reached a crescendo with thousands of women offering ‘pongala’

title: Judicial independence extends to freedom of a judge to dissent or diverge from colleagues, says J. Nagarathna
content: ‘Judicial independence is not exhausted by insulation from the political branches. It also requires that each judge be free to form and express his or her own considered view of the law, even when that view diverges from colleagues’

title: Verdict on bail application of former Travancore Devaswom Board president expected on March 4
content: Having already secured statutory bail last month in a related case involving the theft of gold from the Sabarimala Ayyapa temple’s doorframe, a favorable ruling on Wednesday will pave the way for Padmakumar’s release from prison

title: Holi celebrated with joy in Belagavi
content: The police restricted traffic in the Old City areas like Pangul Galli, Risaldar Galli, Khadak Galli, etc.

title: Seven held for murdering two persons in indiscriminate attack in Nanguneri
content: Two among those arrested have also been named as accused in the attempt to murder Chinnadurai, a Schedule Caste boy, on August 9, 2023

title: PIL seeks removal of ‘Kerala’ from the title of ‘The Kerala Story 2 – Goes Beyond’
content: Petitioners seeks a High Court directive to the Centre and to the CBFC to permit screening of the film with a disclaimer that it was based on a purely fictional theme

title: Garbage woes, building violations take centre stage at east corporation meeting
content: Mr. Rao directs officials to make it mandatory for buildings with more than two floors to place two large dustbins for dry and wet waste

title: ECI set to announce poll dates for West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, Puducherry after Bengal visit
content: The terms of the Assemblies in all these States end between May and June; the schedule could be announced any time after March 10 when the poll body completes its visit to West Bengal

title: Restoration of temple pond in Kurinjipadi a start to citizen-driven revival of Chola-era waterbodies
content: The movement has planned to map historic waterbodies and their interconnections, identify blocked feeder channels and surplus weirs, restore cascading flow patterns between tanks, integrate traditional water wisdom with modern hydrogeological science and establish community-based maintenance models.

title: NGO urges T.N. to make environmental clearance mandatory for large-scale solar projects
content: The NGO stated that the rapid expansion of ground-mounted solar parks is increasingly encroaching on agricultural and grazing lands; it alleged that this practice triggered protests in several districts

title: New genus and species of blind miniature fish discovered in Assam
content: Newly-discovered species of fish, living in a hand-dug well in a village in Assam, has been named Gitchak nakana. Complete absence of a skull roof is its most  bizarre feature. In a departure from almost all other bony fish, the top of its brain is covered only by skin rather than bone

title: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah writes to Tamil Nadu counterpart Stalin backing renewed Centre-State relations debate
content: Siddaramaiah said that it is imperative for all States, irrespective of political affiliations, to join hands in constructive federal dialogue, not to weaken the union but to right-size it

title: CPI(M) condemns U.S.-Israel attack on Iran
content: Questioning whether the Prime Minister has prior knowledge of the strike, CPI(M) leader Raghavulu accuses the TDP-JSP combine and YSRCP of remaining silent on the issue

title: A spices route to tribal empowerment
content: Jayakumar, a  Muthuvan tribal youth from the Mulakuthara settlement, opens a spices shop at Chattupara, near Adimaly. His mission is to ensure fair price and reliable market for farmers in the settlement

title: Khamenei killing: Five geopolitical factors behind India’s silence
content: As the Opposition targets the government for failing to condole the Iranian leader’s death, and of the bombings of hospitals and schools in Iran, officials cite India’s stakes in the region

title: Partial reopening of airspace brings respite to stranded passengers
content: Kerala received over a dozen flights from different locations in West Asia, mainly carrying stranded passengers

title: Paturu handloom cluster revival to benefit 200 weaver families
content: Union government sanctions ₹76.32 lakh for reviving the handloom cluster; 84 weaver families get modern equipment worth ₹28 lakh in the first phase

title: Railways to run 56 Holi special trains
content: South Western Railway is operating 856 trips till March 31 across various railway zones to ensure smooth, safe travelling and also, to avoid rush

title: A.P., Rapido launch ‘driver-cum-guide’ tourism initiative in Vijayawada
content: The initiative aims to transform autorickshaw and cab drivers from mere transporters of passengers to cultural ambassadors who explain the history and heritage of the region to tourists

title: Sericulture experience park inaugurated near Coonoor in the Nilgiris
content: The park reflects the “Soil to Silk” concept and showcases the entire process — from mulberry cultivation to silk saree production — in a demonstrative and educational manner.

title: Four arrested after man gives supari for wife’s murder, makes it look like an accident
content: Video footage of the accident captured on the dashcam of a KSRTC bus forced the police to suspect it to be foul play

title: ₹490 crore lift irrigation project proposed to address drinking water crisis in Hanur
content: The project envisages drawing water from the Cauvery near Saragur village in Kollegal taluk and pumping it to the Uduthorehalla reservoir in Hanur taluk. From there, water will be supplied to tanks, lakes and check dams in Kurattihosuru, Santekane and surrounding areas

title: Fruits of welfare schemes should reach all the eligible people: CM
content: Lapses in the implementation of Praja Palana – Pragati Pranalika will not be tolerated, warns Revanth Reddy

title: Officer pays surprise visit to PU exam centres in Dharwad
content: He says that webcasting facility has been set up at the ZP office to monitor activities in all 43 examination centres in the district
